name: Gitpod API
what_it_does: Gitpod's public REST API for programmatically managing cloud development environments (CDEs), including workspace lifecycle, organization administration, SSH access, and environment variable configuration.
use_cases: Spinning up pre-configured development workspaces for a repository automatically in CI pipelines
Managing and listing active workspaces for a team or organization
Injecting environment variables into workspaces programmatically for secrets management
Starting and stopping workspaces on demand to control cloud compute costs
Retrieving workspace SSH credentials so agents can connect and execute code remotely
not_for: Running arbitrary code directly — Gitpod provisions environments, it does not execute code for you
Self-hosted Gitpod installations on older Community Edition (CE) versions with limited API support
Real-time streaming of workspace logs or terminal output via this REST API
best_when: An agent needs to provision, configure, or tear down ephemeral cloud development environments tied to specific git branches or PRs.
avoid_when: You need a simpler hosted compute environment without the full CDE model; consider GitHub Codespaces or Replit instead.

auth.notes: Access tokens obtained via OAuth2 PKCE flow or created from user settings. Tokens are workspace-scoped. Bearer token passed in Authorization header. Organization-level PATs available for admin workflows.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50 hours/month of workspace usage on Free plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Teams plan from $9/user/month with more hours and private repos; Pro plan for heavier usage
pricing.notes: Free plan is generous for occasional use. API access is available across all plans. Pricing changed significantly in 2024 with the move to Gitpod Flex.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; inferred to be moderate (100-500 req/min for management APIs)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation covers core workspace operations but is inconsistently maintained alongside product changes. Error responses follow gRPC-style status codes with descriptions, but agent-facing REST errors lack detailed remediation guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE operations are idempotent. Workspace creation is not — re-submitting the same context URL creates a new workspace each time. No idempotency key mechanism documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace startup time is 30-120 seconds; agents must poll status rather than assume immediate availability
Context URLs must be fully valid Git URLs — partial refs or SSH URLs may silently fail
Workspaces auto-timeout after inactivity (configurable, default 30 min) and will be stopped mid-task
API endpoints changed between Gitpod Classic and Gitpod Flex — ensure you target the correct base URL
Environment variables set via API are not immediately reflected in running workspaces; restart required

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all API endpoints. OAuth2 PKCE flow is the recommended path. Scopes are documented but coarse-grained (workspace read vs write vs admin). Secrets stored as environment variables in workspaces — encrypted at rest but visible to workspace processes. SOC2 Type II certified.
